title: helicteres oblongifolia
intro: Helicteres oblongifolia, also known as the boxhook or hairy helicteres, is a plant species native to India and other parts of the world, characterized by its unique boxhook-shaped leaves and small, yellowish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its medicinal properties attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Helicteres oblongifolia include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, diarrhea, and rheumatism, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or powder, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
